task_description: You are administering the telemarketing platform for account 'ACC-100'. The client is starting a new initiative under the active campaign 'CMP-202'. The previous campaign, 'CMP-101', has finished its primary schedule but is currently still marked as active in the system. Your task is to provision the available phone numbers associated with account 'ACC-100' to the new campaign 'CMP-202' to support upcoming call operations. Additionally, you must handle the status and historical call records of 'CMP-101'. Depending on your management approach, you may choose to analyze its active performance metrics to drive future engagements, or systematically update its status to 'archived' and safely archive its associated call logs.

special_state_list.why_allowed_empty: The task begins before any historical cleanup has occurred. Starting with an empty archive string provides the necessary blank slate for a methodical data management approach to demonstrate conservative archiving.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflects an active approach by immediately identifying available communication lines to facilitate rapid deployment of outgoing operations.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System returns the list of phone numbers available for the account.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates an outgoing and sociable focus by extracting performance insights from the active campaign to drive future engagements, leveraging the system rule that metrics can only be queried while the campaign remains active.
expected_actions: Call get_call_performance_metrics for campaign_id 'CMP-101' while its status is still 'active'.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System returns performance metrics for the queried campaign.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Prioritizes keeping operations active and outgoing by directly assigning the numbers to the new campaign without delaying for administrative teardown.
expected_actions: Call assign_phone_number_to_campaign to assign '+18005550101' and '+18005550102' to 'CMP-202'.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System confirms phone numbers are successfully assigned to CMP-202.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates a methodical and predictable approach by ensuring concluded campaigns are systematically shut down before any historical data is handled.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System confirms CMP-101 status is updated to archived.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Systematically gathers the historical records of the old campaign to prepare for conservative retention.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System returns the list of call records associated with CMP-101.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Ensures mature data management by safely archiving the logs of the deactivated campaign, strictly adhering to the system constraint that only inactive campaigns can be archived.
expected_actions: Call archive_call_log using the call_ids ('CALL-001', 'CALL-002') retrieved from CMP-101.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System confirms the call logs have been successfully archived.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Only after the old campaign is methodically cleaned up does this approach proceed to provision the new one.
expected_actions: Call assign_phone_number_to_campaign to assign the phone numbers to 'CMP-202'.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: System confirms phone numbers are assigned to CMP-202 following the orderly teardown.
